steps.title: Set up and clear the fraction
steps.content:
The area of a triangle is $\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}$. Since Area = $x^2$, base = $2x + 26$, and height = $x - 12$:
$$x^2 = \frac{1}{2}(2x + 26)(x - 12)$$
Multiply both sides by 2 to eliminate the fraction:
$$2x^2 = (2x + 26)(x - 12)$$

steps.title: Expand and solve
steps.content:
Distribute to expand the right side:
$$(2x + 26)(x - 12) = 2x^2 - 24x + 26x - 312 = 2x^2 + 2x - 312$$
So the equation becomes:
$$2x^2 = 2x^2 + 2x - 312$$
Subtract $2x^2$ from both sides-the quadratic terms cancel:
$$0 = 2x - 312$$
Divide by 2:
$$x = 156$$
